snippet: Biometric information is a record of unique human physiological features or behavioural attributes. Some biometric information can also fall within the definition of health information under the Health Records Act 2001. Biometric information can include a record of a person’s: Biometric information scanning is increasingly used by organisations to confirm identity. Some new technologies even capture biometric information as part of their standard functionality. For example, biometric characteristics can be scanned via: As biometric information is unique to the individual, it has an ongoing identifiable connection to the person. Therefore, schools must consider carefully before biometric technology is introduced and information from children, families and staff is collected and used. Biometrics can be seen as intrusive when Victorian privacy law indicates that the least intrusive method should be preferred.
